title: cornus occidentalis
intro: Cornus occidentalis, also known as Western Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cornus occidentalis are attributed to its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried and used in capsule or powder form, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy.
